what_it_does: This file documents the current locations and operational status of the Upward Engine's memory and pipeline components: the recollection outer-memory chain, the Dragnet catch-all chain, the company's live inner recall, and the wizard-run-1 seed. It marks each item as verified in the current session or as a pointer to another session.
observations: Recollection is built and was backfilled once, then parked; it has no live caller and several 'organs awaiting switch-on' such as distill extraction, ratify, distilled-unit embedding, L3 rollup, the :8008 reranker, and MCP registration.
Recollection's code and data were moved into /home/tim/company this session, old paths were removed, 8,218 DB paths were rewritten, and recall was verified working at the new path.
The company's inner recall (mcp__company__session_recall) is the only live, registered tool today, using the substrate-claude-sessions index and a reranker that is currently down.
Dragnet is described as one chain inside the same compositional system, not a separate system; its company-side files are verified present, but the live build runs in another session.
The wizard-run-1 folder is a by-hand prototype that actually pumped a real project through the model fleet; it is preserved whole as an unmined seed.
The file explicitly notes that the ELSEWHERE pointers were not opened this session and are named so they can be found via session_recall.
standouts: Recollection is the most complete but parked component, with distilled units not embedded being the keystone gap for the UI's multi-space map.
Inner session recall is the only actually live and registered recall tool right now.
Wizard-run-1 is the only real project that has been run through the model fleet and has not yet been mined.
concerns: A 'PARKED' system with multiple unactivated parts risks stale state if the dedicated session does not resume soon.
Live state for Dragnet and the recollection design session lives outside this file, so the map may drift from actual progress.
The reranker being down is noted but not framed as a tracked issue or blocker.
Absolute paths and session IDs may become stale quickly.
conventions: Uses a YAML frontmatter state register with type, register, aliases, tags, status, coverage, and relates-to.
Uses VERIFIED and ELSEWHERE markers to separate session-verified facts from cross-session pointers.
Uses wiki-style internal links to related design and planning documents.
Includes an honesty disclaimer about which pointers were not opened this session.
Mixes neutral description with evaluative status labels such as PARKED and 'organs awaiting switch-on'.
notes: A grounded orientation map of where the Upward Engine's memory chains, catch-all pipeline, and prototype seed live, with explicit honesty about what was verified in-session versus pointed elsewhere.
questions: What event or approval is required to unpark recollection and switch on its awaiting organs?
What is the exact freshness boundary at which recollection (outer/deep) is meant to union with inner session recall?
What specific models and endpoints constitute the 'model fleet' used in wizard-run-1?
Who is Tim in this context and what is the Tim-approval workflow for ratify?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide an honest, grounded map of system state and locations, and the file does exactly that while also flagging activation gaps and cross-session pointers.
apparent_themes: Grounded state documentation over aspirational claims.
Distinction between outer/cross-session memory and inner/single-session recall.
Compositional chains where Dragnet is one chain among others.
Preservation of a prototype run as a seed for future systems.
Dogfooding on the company's own corpus to recall the system's own making.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a single trustworthy map that future work can use to locate components, know what is live versus parked, and understand which state lives in other sessions; it also signals the next activation steps for recollection.
novelty: The explicit VERIFIED/ELSEWHERE epistemic markers in a system-state ledger, and the treatment of a one-off prototype run as an intentionally unmined seed rather than a finished deliverable.
contribution: It contributes a location and status map with a clear verification boundary for the Upward Engine's distributed memory and pipeline components, serving as an orientation anchor and handoff surface without claiming primacy over other documents.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a descriptive state ledger mapping the verified locations and operational status of the Upward Engine's recollection outer-memory chain, Dragnet catch-all chain, live inner session recall, and the wizard-run-1 seed, distinguishing what was verified in-session from what lives in other sessions.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to create an honest, grounded orientation map for navigating and continuing work across the Upward Engine's distributed components.
